---
title: "12 step alternatives"
format: html
---

# Alternatives to 12-Step Programs

While 12-Step programs help many people, there are other options that focus on different approaches.

These programs still provide support, structure, and community.

::: callout
### Examples include:

-   **SMART Recovery**\
    Focus on cognitive-behavioral skills and self-management

-   **Women for Sobriety**\
    Focus on emotional growth and empowerment

-   **Secular Organizations for Sobriety (SOS)**\
    Emphasizes personal responsibility without spirituality

-   **JACS (Jewish recovery support)**\
    Combines recovery with cultural and spiritual identity

-   **Faith-based or community groups**\
    Found in churches, mosques, temples, and other settings\
:::

## Choosing What Works for You

Different approaches work for different people.

What matters most:

-   Building support\
-   Staying accountable\
-   Maintaining abstinence

## Questions to Consider

-   Have you ever attended a 12-Step meeting? What was it like?
-   Have you tried other recovery groups?
-   Do you plan to attend meetings? Where and when?
-   How could meetings help you stay sober?
-   Are there alternative supports you want to explore?

## Your Plan

-   Meeting type I will try: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\
-   When I will go: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\
-   Support person I can contact: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

**Remember: There are many pathways to recovery—the key is finding one that keeps you connected and supported.**

![](https://github.com/jjcurtin/aud_support/blob/main/modules/images/supportgroup.png?raw=true){fig-align="center"}
